title: mentha longifolia subsp. minutiflora
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. minutiflora, also known as dwarf or small-flowered horse mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that belongs to the genus Mentha, a hardy perennial herb with fragrant leaves and purple flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have been used to treat digestive issues and ease respiratory problems. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia subsp. minutiflora include its ability to soothe stomach upset, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, including essential oils and tinctures, which are said to promote relaxation and relieve stress.
